← All worlds/World 09
World 09 · 1 state

Inner Peace

Films for the days that don’t need to be saved or solved — only inhabited. The slow art of letting the world be enough.

Choose an emotion
Slowing Down
On Slowing Down

At some point — usually quietly, without announcement — you realise you have been in a hurry for years. Not going anywhere particular. Just moving fast, because speed felt like proof of something. These films are for the moment that speed runs out of argument. They are slow films. Not dull — slow with intention, the way a master craftsperson works with care instead of urgency. They show a life attended to rather than raced through. They are not prescriptions for how to live. They are invitations to notice that the present moment, patiently inhabited, is already enough.

Slowing Down

Films for the day you stop arguing with the present.

CS9.3
2023

Perfect Days

Wim Wenders

View Film →

A Tokyo public-toilet cleaner lives a routine of cassette tapes, paperbacks, and small kindnesses. The film follows him quietly through several days.

CS8.7
2016

Paterson

Jim Jarmusch

View Film →

A bus driver in Paterson, New Jersey, named Paterson, writes poems between routes. The film follows seven days of his quiet, devoted life.

CS8.7
2017

Columbus

Kogonada

View Film →

A young woman caring for her mother in Columbus, Indiana, befriends a Korean-American man waiting on his dying father — and they talk, slowly, about everything.

CS9.0
1995

Before Sunrise

Richard Linklater

View Film →

A young American man and a young French woman meet on a train, decide to spend one night walking around Vienna together, and talk until dawn.

CS8.4
2007

Into the Wild

Sean Penn

View Film →

A young man abandons his possessions, savings, and family after graduating, hitchhiking toward Alaska in search of an honest life. Based on a true story.

CS8.8
2020

Minari

Lee Isaac Chung

View Film →

A Korean-American family moves to rural Arkansas in the 1980s to start a farm. The film is a slow, attentive portrait of building a life in unfamiliar soil.

CS8.9
1999

The Straight Story

David Lynch

View Film →

A seventy-three-year-old man drives a lawnmower across two American states to visit his estranged, ailing brother. Based on a true story.

CS9.1
1988

My Neighbor Totoro

Hayao Miyazaki

View Film →

Two young sisters move to the countryside with their father to be closer to their hospitalised mother, and find the woods near their house are full of friendly spirits.

CS8.7
2003

Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter… and Spring

Kim Ki-duk

View Film →

A young monk’s life unfolds across five seasons in a tiny Buddhist temple that floats on a remote Korean lake.

CS8.3
2021

After Yang

Kogonada

View Film →

When the family’s android “brother” breaks down, a father tries to repair him — and discovers, in the process, a record of small everyday moments.

CS9.5
1953

Tokyo Story

Yasujirō Ozu

View Film →

An elderly couple travel from rural Japan to Tokyo to visit their adult children. They find the children too busy to make time for them.

CS9.3
1955

Pather Panchali

Satyajit Ray

View Film →

A poor rural family in Bengal — a father who dreams of writing, a mother managing the household, and two children growing up.

CS9.5
2000

Yi Yi

Edward Yang

View Film →

A Taipei family across three generations navigates a year of life. Nobody is the protagonist; life itself is.

CS8.5
2019

A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood

Marielle Heller

View Film →

A cynical journalist is assigned to profile Fred Rogers and finds himself disarmed by a man who says exactly what he means.

CS8.8
2006

Once

John Carney

View Film →

A street musician and a Czech immigrant woman make music together over a week in Dublin.

CS8.8
2007

The Band's Visit

Eran Kolirin

View Film →

An Egyptian police orchestra is stranded in a small Israeli desert town. The night they spend changes everyone.

CS8.9
1987

Babette's Feast

Gabriel Axel

View Film →

A French refugee housekeeper spends her lottery winnings on one extraordinary meal for the austere Danish village that sheltered her.

CS8.9
1985

Tampopo

Jūzō Itami

View Film →

A truck driver helps a widow improve her ramen shop. Surrounding this story are vignettes about food and desire.

CS8.7
2015

Chef's Table Season 1

David Gelb

View Film →

Six chefs across the world discuss their philosophy of food and their process of becoming who they are.

CS8.9
1999

The Straight Story

David Lynch

View Film →

A seventy-three-year-old man drives a lawnmower across Iowa and Wisconsin to visit his ailing brother.

CS9.2
1993

Three Colors: Blue

Krzysztof Kieślowski

View Film →

A woman who loses her family in a car crash tries to live as if she has no connections to anyone or anything.

CS8.0
2018

Ramen Shop

Eric Khoo

View Film →

A young Japanese chef travels to Singapore to find his mother’s side of the family and reconcile with her memory through food.

CS8.9
2011

Jiro Dreams of Sushi

David Gelb

View Film →

An eighty-five-year-old sushi master in a Tokyo subway restaurant has three Michelin stars and is still trying to improve.

CS8.8
2008

Waltz with Bashir

Ari Folman

View Film →

An animated documentary: a filmmaker investigates his own missing memories from the 1982 Lebanon War.

Spiritual Awakening →

For the part of you that’s bigger than you.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.

18 emotional states·277 curated films·Growing weekly
